mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.25% signals monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Higher discount rates compress present values of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) in months with PAYEMS growth > 150k versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,900 thousand units combined with retail sales above 360,000 million dollars signals strong aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because both demand components must be elevated simultaneously to confirm sustained economic momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1933.0 and RSAFS at 365369.0 as of 2006-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong housing demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST > 1900 AND RSAFS > 360000, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins. This contraction predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.2%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer purchasing power and corporate hiring expectations. This threshold crossing predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) when UNRATE > 4.8% versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation above 2.5% year-over-year combined with federal funds rate above 4.0% signals the Federal Reserve is in active tightening mode with real rates elevated. This joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because restrictive monetary policy constrains equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.7 and FEDFUNDS at 4.29% as of 2006-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding at an annualized rate above 3.0% signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations. This always-on trend signal predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11233.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2006-10-15
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) in quarters with GDPC1 growth > 3.0% annualized versus baseline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales declining month-over-month combined with housing starts falling below 1,850 thousand units signals simultaneous contraction in consumer and housing demand. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days because both demand pillars must weaken together to confirm demand-side recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 365369.0 million and HOUST at 1933.0 thousand as of 2006-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS declines > 1.0% MoM AND HOUST < 1850, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
